title: Bias & subgroup reporting standard (required breakdowns + gap statements)
context: Underreporting of subgroup performance in cancer ML models is a known and high-risk problem: models may perform very differently across sites/scanners, sexes, age bands, or ancestry groups, but these breakdowns are rarely published. Without a standard, model card contributors would apply inconsistent rules about when to report, estimate, or omit subgroup metrics. Depends on schema-001.
objective: Define and publish the bias and subgroup reporting standard that every model card must satisfy: specifying required subgroup dimensions, mandating explicit gap statements when data is unavailable, and defining the verified enum used to distinguish reported, reproduced, reported-unverified, and not-reported metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: Standard enumerates required subgroup dimensions where available: imaging site/scanner, sex, age band, and ancestry/race (when ethically reported by the original study), plus the headline and calibration metrics expected for each.

Standard mandates that every card either reports subgroup metrics with cited sources OR carries an explicit, sourced 'subgroup performance not reported' gap statement — no silent omission is permitted.

Standard defines the verified enum: reported (authors reported; cited), reproduced (harness verified on open eval data), reported-unverified (cited but not independently verified), not-reported (absent from all sources); specifies when each value applies.
Standard specifies when reproduction via the subgroup-metric harness is required (open eval data available) versus citing the authors' numbers (closed/unavailable eval data).
Standard is reviewed and approved by the Data-License+Privacy reviewer and the ML/technical reviewer.
Document is committed to the project repo and referenced from the canonical schema.
resources: PLAN.md — Solution approach, Metrics & bias pipeline step
Mitchell et al., Model Cards for Model Reporting (2019)
output: A committed bias and subgroup reporting standard specifying required dimensions, gap-statement rules, and the verified enum, for use by all model card tasks.
